solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String reverseStr(String s, int k) {
        char[] chars = s.toCharArray();
        for (int i = 0; i < chars.length; i += 2 * k) {
            int left = i;
            int right = Math.min(i + k - 1, chars.length - 1);
            while (left < right) {
                char temp = chars[left];
                chars[right] = chars[left];
                chars[left] = temp;
                left++;
                right--;
            }
        }
        return new String(chars);
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution processes the string in chunks of 2k characters. For each chunk, it reverses the first k characters. This is done in-place on a character array for efficiency, and the reversal is handled with a two-pointer approach. The algorithm ensures that all edge cases (e.g., fewer than k characters remaining) are naturally handled by using `Math.min()` to clamp the right index.
